programme.title: Premier League 360 - Big Sam's Bolton

programme.title: Giroud
programme.desc: A profile of footballer Olivier Giroud, who is France's all-time leading goalscorer and has played for Arsenal, Chelsea and AC Milan.

programme.title: Secret World of Sound with David Attenborough
programme.desc: From melodic songs to strange hums, David Attenborough shares the sounds that gibbons, lyrebirds, bull elk and more use to find a mate - and warn off the competition.

programme.title: Secret World of Sound with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A look at how baby animals communicate in their quest for survival, using sound to beg and call for help in the early days of life.

programme.title: High Life in the Dead Mountains
programme.desc: A look at the stark karst plateau of caves and dry peaks in Austria's Totes Gebirge, hiding rich ecosystem where wildlife thrives against the odds.

programme.title: Wild Science
programme.desc: Using CGI, archive footage and science, to witness how creatures from deep seas to land predators hunt, survive and dominate nature.

programme.title: Matsalu Moose: Wild Giants of the Baltics
programme.desc: Matsalu National Park at the Baltic Sea is known for being one of Europe's most important stopover places for migrating birds. However, few know that an exceptionally large moose colony also lives here. This documentary follows the moose through the eyes of two yearlings as they are chased away from their mother before she gives birth to the next calf.

programme.title: How the Wild Things Sleep
programme.desc: The secrets of the most extreme sleepers of the animal kingdom, looking at how they deal with sleep deprivation and trying to discover whether animals dream.

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: The awe-inspiring beauty of Gros Morne National Park, a 1,100-mile expanse located on the western coast of Canada's Newfoundland.

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: Chile's Atacama Desert, which stretches for 600 miles and is the world's driest non-polar region. From menacing volcanoes to resplendent flamingos, it is truly unique.

programme.title: The Wild Wild East

programme.title: The Wild Wild East

programme.title: The Wild Wild East

programme.title: Waterworld Africa
programme.desc: Following the wildlife of a South African mangrove forest through the changing seasons, meeting the red-claw mangrove crabs that keep the forest clean.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The next stage of lemur introductions keeps the primate care staff on their toes, while gibbons Kim and Tien head back to their familiar stomping ground. Tuans orangutans keep it cool with giant ice lollies.

programme.desc: In Dao Tien a group of misbehaving doucs need to be relocated and female golden-cheeked gibbon Tungs release is hampered by the weather. While rain doesn't stop gibbons Kim and Tien going out into their tree filled enclosure.

programme.desc: Mark and the guys wade in water to save a dilapidated springhouse, then they rebuild it back at the bone yard.

programme.desc: Chase and the crew are tasked with the relocation and land manifestation of a yurt on an island on Togus Pond; the team seeks to beat the ice and snow so they can have the yurt ready for the family to drop their fishing lines in the frozen pond.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and the team seek to transform a family's 1950s lobster shack into a cabin that will create a lifetime of memories for generations to come; they re-imagine the cabin's interior layout, preserve a mural and add an exterior widow's walk.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A couple inherit a quaint cabin; Chase and his team focus on transforming the former fish and lobster shack into a fun family cabin.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: In Oakland, Maine, Chase and his team take on a project on McGrath Pond that has been empty for 15 years; while it seems like a pretty straightforward renovation, starting a project this late in the season raises a lot of challenges.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A family has created lasting memories of their cabin overlooking Harpswell Sound, but the cabin is in need of some work to see the next 20 years and beyond; Chase and the team expand it to accommodate the next generation.
